reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_230]]
Speaker 3: As we come to the end of today’s interview, I’d like to invite both of you to speak directly to the camera and share a few words with the students watching.

[[BLOCK:srt_231]]
Speaker 3: Perhaps a brief welcome.

[[BLOCK:srt_232]]
Speaker 3: Or a few words of encouragement.

[[BLOCK:srt_233]]
Speaker 3: Now, let’s move on to the next question.

[[BLOCK:srt_234]]
Speaker 3: This weekend, we’ll also be celebrating in China with the DVA workshop and the commencement ceremony.

[[BLOCK:srt_235]]
Speaker 3: For the Chinese students graduating there, would you like to share any words of congratulations or encouragement?

[[BLOCK:srt_236]]
Speaker 1: I don’t have anything to add.

[[BLOCK:srt_237]]
Speaker 2: As we’ve said several times, when you graduate from Golden Gate University, you’re not leaving—you’re officially becoming part of our global alumni network.

[[BLOCK:srt_238]]
Speaker 2: We hope you’ll stay in touch, continue to apply the knowledge and skills you’ve gained, and remain connected with one another, because you’re now part of a vibrant global community.

[[BLOCK:srt_239]]
Speaker 2: This is also the time to start giving back by supporting those who come after you. We hope you’ll maintain a close connection with the university and continue sharing your valuable ideas and suggestions with us. Perhaps some of you will even return one day as faculty members or professors.

[[BLOCK:srt_240]]
Speaker 2: So you’re not leaving.

[[BLOCK:srt_241]]
Speaker 2: You’re simply becoming part of the larger Golden Gate University global family.
